tools.title: 16" Macbook Pro, M4 Pro
tools.description: 48 GB RAM (2024). The primary coding device. Where I mostly watch claude, codex, cursor, opencode and more cli tools write code across multiple worktrees.

tools.title: 16" MacBook Pro, M2 Pro
tools.description: 32GB RAM (2023). I prepare tech talks, focus on my hobbies, write novels & all sorts of passion projects in this machine.

tools.title: 14" MacBook Pro, M2 Pro
tools.description: 32GB RAM (2023). The headless 24x7 machine that runs my plex media server, self hosted apps & AI Agents - currently using Hermes Agent

tools.title: 4K 32" LG UltraFine Ergo
tools.description: Bigger the display, bigger the motivation to use it to read your code. The ergonomic stand is chef's kiss.

tools.title: Keychron K4 Wireless
tools.description: Mechanical keyboards are love. The satisfying clicks of the normal profile Keychron is the reason I code most of the time.

tools.title: Logitech MX Master 3
tools.description: As a power user of VSCode and tmux, I rarely need to click stuff. But when I do, I do it with the most ergonomic mouse.

tools.title: Greensoul Imperium Electric
tools.description: The table that acts as my workbench where I usually code, write and do all the tasks

tools.title: Sennheiser HD 458BT
tools.description: Most trusted headphones for work — video calls, egghead lessons, podcasts while coding. Can't live without this one.

tools.title: Zed
tools.description: With me working on multiple projects the norm, Zed provides the native performance and seamless switching of projects. Keeping coding sessions productive despite the size of the projects active.

tools.title: Oh My Zsh!
tools.description: Can't use my terminal without installing Oh My Zsh & setting up my favorite theme. Who wants to cd when you can just type the directory name!

tools.title: Ghostty
tools.description: The trusted terminal emulator on which most of my work is done. Its fast, configurable and gets the jobs done.

tools.title: dmux
tools.description: Helps run parallel agents across multiple worktrees for the same project and helps manage multiple projects. Has been the biggest productivity booster - powered by tmux

tools.title: CodexBar
tools.description: Shows realtime token usage stats directly on the menu bar.

tools.title: Paper
tools.description: My go-to tool now for creating designs powered by AI. Helps converting my ideas to designs then to code a seamless process

tools.title: Excalidraw
tools.description: Professional quality scribbling & a very funny logo maker. My preferred design tool for quick ideas.

tools.title: Tiles
tools.description: By removing the use of mouse to resize & move windows, I'm 2x more efficient but also 2x less efficient deciding which window goes where.

tools.title: Notion
tools.description: Pretty much all my ideas, projects, plans & thoughts about my digital life are recorded in the generous free tier.

tools.title: Spark
tools.description: Keeping my inbox clean by only reading the messages filtered by this great email app.

tools.title: Cron
tools.description: They say it's extremely powerful for managing calendars, but I use it because its event reminders are so much better than the native Mac calendar.

tools.title: Simplenote
tools.description: Replacing the need for paper in my life. Works across all my devices — take a quick note whenever you want & forget about it forever.
